title: Bridge Essay: Behavioral inflexibility through overtraining is mediated by reduced mGluR1/5 si

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**Behavioral inflexibility through overtraining is mediated by reduced mGluR1/5 signaling capacity in the dorsolateral striatum.**. PLoS Biol.. PMID: 40729367. DOI: 10.1371/journal.pbio.3003288. The control of instrumental actions engages distinct behavioral strategies whose contributions are regulated with experience. Instrumental performance, which depends on the causal relationship between actions and their outcomes (A-O), relies on flexible, goal-directed control of behavior. Actions can become less sensitive to changes in action-outcome (A-O) contingencies with repetition, resulting in more inflexible, habitual behaviors. The loss of flexibility with repetition requires plasticity
